item.title: Turkey Officials Tell Investors War May Dent Growth, Lift Prices
item.description: Turkey’s top economic policymakers told investors this week that the fallout from the Iran war is likely to weigh on Turkish growth and raise price pressures in the short term, according to people with knowledge of the conversations.

item.title: Uranium Royalty to Buy Sweetwater in $1.1 Billion Nuclear Fuel Deal
item.description: Uranium Royalty Corp. agreed to buy Sweetwater Royalties for about $1.1 billion in a deal that would create a new US-listed company to capitalize on growing demand for nuclear fuel.

item.title: Private Credit Funds Face More Pressure as Banks Swap Collateral
item.description: For years, Wall Street banks eagerly helped private credit funds amplify their investing firepower with hundreds of billions of dollars in loans, helping them notch ever-higher returns.

item.title: South Africa Has Room for $732 Million Fuel Relief Extension, Citi Says
item.description: South Africa has enough fiscal space to extend a fuel-tax cut by two months to cushion consumers from an oil shock triggered by the Iran war, Citigroup Inc. said.

item.title: SEC Asks for Input on Slimming CAT Trade Data Collection, Costs
item.description: The Securities and Exchange Commission asked for industry input on how to trim down the cost and scope of trading data that exchanges and brokers report to a centralized database.

item.title: US Factory Output Rebound in First Quarter Extended Beyond AI
item.description: US factory output snapped back in the first quarter after disappointing at the end of 2025, and the advance extended beyond the artificial intelligence build-out.

item.title: ECB’s Schnabel Says It’s Good Time to Discuss Euro Bonds Again
item.description: European Central Bank Executive Board member Isabel Schnabel said now is a “good time” to again debate joint European Union borrowing.

item.title: IMF Says Egypt Has Built Buffers to Tackle Shocks as Pound Gains
item.description: The International Monetary Fund said Egypt’s economic reforms and stronger buffers are enabling the country to better weather external shocks, as the local pound reverses some losses after the Iran war ceasefire.

item.title: Hacked Crypto Platform to Relaunch After Securing New Funding
item.description: Decentralized finance platform Drift Protocol, the victim of an exploit earlier this month where hackers stole approximately $285 million of users’ cryptocurrency, plans to relaunch after securing around $150 million in funding from a consortium led by stablecoin issuer Tether Holdings SA.

item.title: Anthropic Unveils Updated Opus Model Aimed at Advanced Coding
item.description: Anthropic PBC is introducing an updated version of its most powerful, widely available AI model, barely a week after it limited the release of a more advanced offering called Mythos.

item.title: IMF Slashes Growth Outlook for Gulf Oil States on Iran War Shock
item.description: The International Monetary Fund cut growth forecasts for countries across the Middle East due to disruptions stemming from the Iran war and warned that a prolonged conflict could put further pressure on the region’s economies.

item.title: Bank of Ireland Proposes Ditching London Listing to Save Costs
item.description: Bank of Ireland Group Plc is proposing delisting from the London Stock Exchange as it looks to control costs, citing low trading in its shares on the exchange.

item.title: Portugal Sells Offshore Yuan Bonds in First For Euro-Area
item.description: Portugal has become the first euro-area country to tap the offshore renminbi bond market, raising the equivalent of about €250 million as it seeks to diversify its funding sources.

item.title: Traders Revive Short-Volatility Bets as Peace Hopes Calm Markets
item.description: As hopes for a lasting deal between the US and Iran steady markets worldwide, traders are tentatively reviving a range of bets that benefit from falling volatility.

item.title: Allbirds Shares Sink as 582% AI Surge Comes to Screeching Halt
item.description: Allbirds Inc.’s surprising pivot from wool sneakers to artificial intelligence infrastructure is looking more like a flash in the pan for the stock than a driver of long-term gains.

item.title: CD&R-Backed Multi-Color Wins Approval of Debt Restructuring
item.description: Private equity backed Multi-Color Corp won court approval for a restructuring plan that cuts nearly $4 billion in debt, clearing the way for the label maker to exit Chapter 11 after striking a deal with a dissenting lender group.

item.title: Bank of England to Add AI Risks Into Stress Tests, Lawmakers Say
item.description: The Bank of England will include risks from artificial intelligence as part of its stress tests on the financial system.

item.title: US Trade Representative to Travel to Mexico for USMCA Talks
item.description: Mexico will host US Trade Representative Jamieson Greer and his team in Mexico City as talks between the two countries continue ahead of a review of the North American USMCA trade pact.

item.title: Mythos Poses Risk to SEC Market-Tracking Database, Group Says
item.description: Anthropic PBC’s new artificial intelligence model Mythos could put traders and the broader financial system at risk through a Securities and Exchange Commission database, the American Securities Association said Thursday.

item.title: Schwab Considering Prediction Markets Linked to Finance Events
item.description: Charles Schwab Corp. is likely to launch prediction markets linked to financial events as competitors including Robinhood Markets Inc. expand their presence in the space.

item.title: Stock Rally Pauses as Traders Weigh Mixed Earnings, Iran War
item.description: Stocks fluctuated Thursday morning as oil prices gained and traders weighed whether a fragile ceasefire in the Iran war may be extended and mixed first-quarter earnings results.

item.title: Bank of America Kicks Off Five-Part Investment-Grade Bond Sale
item.description: A Bank of America Corp. investment-grade bond sale got underway on Thursday, adding to a bevy of offerings by major Wall Street lenders this week.

item.title: WTF Happened to San Francisco?
item.description: A beloved tech founder was found stabbed in the streets of San Francisco. The city took the blame.

item.title: DTEK Oil & Gas Seeks to Tap US-Ukraine Reconstruction Fund
item.description: DTEK Oil & Gas is looking to tap a fund launched as part of the mineral resources deal between the US and Ukraine for investment, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US Industrial Production Fell in March in Broad Decline
item.description: US industrial production declined in March as manufacturing softened and utility output decreased in the wake of the Iran war.

item.title: IMF Sees Iran War Driving African Nations to Seek Greater Help
item.description: Spillovers from the Iran war are sending more African governments to request financial assistance from the International Monetary Fund, as they’re facing rising inflationary and fiscal strains.

item.title: Billionaire Dangote Boosts Jet Fuel to Europe as War Hits Supply
item.description: A mega-refinery owned by Africa’s richest person is becoming an increasingly important source of vital jet fuel supplies to Europe, helping fill a gap left by the impact of the Iran war while boosting profitability.

item.title: Argentina in Talks for $2 Billion Loan Backed by World Bank
item.description: Argentina is negotiating a $2 billion loan with private banks with guarantees from World Bank institutions, according to two people with direct knowledge of the matter.

item.title: Khosla, Ribbit Back College Dropouts’ Startup Slash at $1.4 Billion Valuation
item.description: Slash Financial Inc., a financial services startup run by two 24-year-old founders, has landed a $1.4 billion valuation for its mission to take on larger players like Ramp Inc. and Brex.

item.title: Meta Raises Prices of Quest VR Headsets on Growing Memory Costs
item.description: Meta Platforms Inc. is boosting the prices of its virtual reality headsets because of rising memory chip costs, adding to an industrywide wave of recent increases.
